conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 46, AI-assisted design tools and BIM coordination are standard, but physical construction automation has not advanced materially beyond 2025 capabilities. 3D concrete printing handles simple geometries; bricklaying robots operate on a handful of commercial sites. Humanoid robots remain above $25K and lack the dexterity for framing, roofing, or MEP rough-in. The gap between digital planning capability and physical execution capability remains wide.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 68, AI handles pricing optimization, inventory management, demand forecasting, and online customer service at near-human levels. Cashierless checkout is widespread in grocery and convenience formats. E-commerce product recommendations and search are AI-dominated. Remaining human tasks: in-store merchandising, complex B2B sales relationships, luxury goods consultation, and physical store operations.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 62, AI diagnostic tools (radiology AI, pathology screening, clinical decision support) are integrated into standard workflows but still require physician oversight per regulation. AI scribes handle clinical documentation. Drug discovery pipelines use ML. Remaining gap: physical examination, surgical procedures, bedside manner, mental health therapeutic alliance, and the regulatory requirement for human clinical judgment on treatment decisions.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 37, AI manages back-of-house operations (inventory, scheduling, food cost, demand forecasting) effectively. Self-ordering kiosks and delivery apps handle most customer ordering. Kitchen robots handle specific repetitive prep tasks (frying, drink-making). Remaining gap: most food preparation, table service, housekeeping, and the hospitality interactions that define guest experience remain human.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 44, AI assists with regulatory compliance checking, document processing, surveillance analytics, and administrative case management. Predictive policing tools are deployed but controversial. Remaining gap: discretionary enforcement decisions, courtroom proceedings, diplomatic negotiation, emergency response coordination, and all functions requiring sworn authority, physical presence, or the exercise of state power through human agents.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 48, precision agriculture (GPS-guided tractors, drone crop monitoring, AI yield prediction) is mature in large-scale commercial farming. Autonomous harvesting works for row crops on flat terrain. Fishing vessel automation handles navigation and fish detection. Remaining gap: harvesting of delicate crops (fruit, vegetables), livestock handling, forestry in rough terrain, artisanal fishing, and smallholder farming in developing economies where terrain and plot size preclude automation.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 29, AI tutoring systems provide personalized instruction and assessment, but adoption in formal education is limited by institutional inertia and teacher union resistance. LLMs draft lesson plans and grade routine assignments. The score barely moves from the 28 baseline because the conservative scenario assumes slow institutional adoption and continued strong preference for human teachers in K-12 and university settings.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 32, AI predictive maintenance systems identify equipment failures before they occur across industrial and building contexts. Autonomous floor-cleaning robots are widespread in commercial settings. Smart diagnostics guide human technicians. Remaining gap: the vast majority of maintenance and repair work — plumbing, electrical, HVAC, appliance repair, vehicle maintenance — requires hands-on manipulation in unstructured environments that current robots cannot navigate.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 77, generative AI produces competent visual art, music, video, and written content for most commercial purposes. AI tools are standard in advertising, game design, and media production. The conservative scenario assumes capability growth slows from the 2024-2025 pace but the baseline is already high. Remaining human domain: emotionally resonant long-form narrative, live performance, cultural criticism, and art that derives value from human authorship.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 88, factory automation continues its decades-long trajectory. AI quality inspection (computer vision), predictive maintenance, and supply chain optimization are standard. Collaborative robots handle a wider range of assembly tasks. CNC and additive manufacturing are AI-optimized. Remaining gap: custom/low-volume manufacturing, repair and rework of defective products, and tasks requiring human judgment on material quality and finish standards.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory barriers hold; economic barriers begin modest erosion as IFR deployment curves continue

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 82, AI handles financial analysis, algorithmic trading, fraud detection, credit scoring, insurance underwriting, and routine IT operations autonomously. LLMs draft contracts, regulatory filings, and client reports. IT infrastructure management is heavily automated (AIOps). Remaining gap: complex M&A advisory, strategic financial planning requiring relationship management, novel cybersecurity threats, and regulatory judgment calls.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 54, autonomous trucks operate on highway segments with safety drivers. Warehouse automation (AMRs, robotic picking for regular items) is standard in large fulfillment centers. Drone delivery is limited to specific rural/suburban corridors. Route optimization is AI-dominated. Remaining gap: urban last-mile delivery, loading/unloading of irregular cargo, port and rail operations in complex environments, and all contexts requiring interaction with the public.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 76, AI handles most analytical professional work: legal research, management consulting analysis, engineering design optimization, scientific literature review, and market research. LLMs draft professional documents at associate-level quality. Remaining gap: client relationship management, courtroom advocacy, creative strategy development, and leadership functions that require organizational influence and trust.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 50, incremental gains in prefabrication automation and robotic finishing (painting, sanding) nudge the score up modestly. Humanoid costs have fallen to ~$20K but reliability in outdoor, variable-weather construction environments remains poor. Fully autonomous site work is limited to repetitive tasks on greenfield sites with standardized designs. Renovation, retrofit, and complex architectural work remain firmly human-dependent.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 70, autonomous inventory robots (shelf-scanning, restocking in standardized aisles) are common in large retail. AI-powered virtual sales assistants handle most product inquiries. B2B sales tools automate prospecting and proposal generation. Human value concentrates in relationship-intensive sales, visual merchandising creativity, and managing the physical retail environment.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 68, AI achieves specialist-level diagnostic accuracy across most imaging and lab modalities. Robotic surgery systems (da Vinci successors) handle a wider range of procedures with increasing autonomy. AI manages chronic disease protocols end-to-end. Physical examination, emergency triage, and the interpersonal dimensions of care remain human. Nursing tasks involving patient handling are beyond robotic capability at this cost point.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 48, robotic kitchen stations handle a wider range of standardized food preparation in fast-casual settings. Self-check-in is standard in mid-range hotels. Room-service delivery robots operate in simple hotel corridors. Housekeeping, fine dining service, complex food preparation, and guest relationship management remain firmly human. Humanoid costs (~$20K) still too high relative to hospitality wages.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 50, AI handles most routine administrative government functions (permit processing, benefits eligibility, tax assessment). Legal research and brief drafting are AI-dominated. Surveillance and intelligence analysis heavily AI-augmented. Physical governance (policing, military, emergency services, diplomacy) remains human. Regulatory discretion and accountability require human decision-makers.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 48, the plateau at 48 reflects the hard physical constraints of agricultural automation. Robotic fruit picking improves incrementally but remains slow and expensive relative to manual labor globally. Livestock management robots handle feeding and milking but not veterinary care or complex herding. The vast global agricultural workforce is in smallholder contexts where automation economics don't work.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 35, AI tutors are standard supplementary tools in well-resourced schools. Automated assessment handles most objective and short-answer formats. AI teaching assistants manage routine student queries. Human teachers remain central for classroom management, emotional support, hands-on instruction, and the relational dimension of education. Physical education, arts, and lab sciences are minimally affected.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 32, the plateau at 32 confirms that physical repair capabilities have not advanced materially. AI diagnosis is excellent — it can tell you what's wrong — but fixing it still requires human hands. Humanoid robots at ~$20K lack the fine manipulation and tool use needed for repair work. The territory score reflects this stark cognitive-physical gap.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 80, AI generates feature-length video content, composes orchestral scores, and produces publishable novels of acceptable commercial quality. Human creators focus on high-art contexts where provenance matters, live performance, and curatorial roles that shape cultural discourse. The 80 reflects near-complete technical capability for commercial creative production.
conservative_replaceability.barrier_erosion_note: Regulatory, economic, and human-preference barriers erode progressively with scenario intensity

conservative_replaceability.primary_mechanism: At 92, lights-out manufacturing is achievable for standardized high-volume products. AI manages entire production lines including adaptive process control.
